description.text:
Closes #4559 

- [X] I documented all behaviour within the code as far as I could.
- [X] This PR targets the develop branch and *not* the master branch.
- [X] I have tested my changes extensively and paid extra attention to potential cross-platform issues (e./g. Cmd/Ctrl/Super key bindings) 
- [X] I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
- [ ] I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md.
- [X] I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
- [X] I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
- [X] In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing files for examples)
- [X] Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge issues

<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.

  First, the obligatory checklist. You must make sure to follow this list as
  much as possible to make merging your PR as easy as possible:

  1. I documented all behaviour within the code as far as I could.
  2. This PR targets the develop branch and *not* the master branch.
  3. I have tested my changes extensively and paid extra attention to potential
     cross-platform issues (e./g. Cmd/Ctrl/Super key bindings)
  4. I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
  5. I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md
  6. I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
  7. I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
  8. In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing
     files for examples)
  9. Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge
     issues

  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->

Move the logic that applies the background color to inline-code away from the CSS and into the background layer logic.
 
## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->

Following feedback from @nathanlesage in #4560 I opened a new PR with the following changes:

- Renamed `codeblock-background.ts` to  `code-background.ts`  and added a condition to consider these rules considering `InlineCode` in addition to `CodeText`
- Renamed CSS rules to match these changes. That is, changing the CSS class to `code-line-background` and removing the background color rule applying for `cm-monospace` for consistency with the inline and code styles.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->

Tested on: Linux (Ubuntu 23.04), and macOS Ventura 13

closed_issues.summary: CSS class `cm-selectionBackground` is not visible when selecting code inside a `plain`, `text` or empty code block

description.text:
### Description The base theme `berlin`, from which the other take these CSS rules, does not highlight characters when selecting text inside an inline code word, like `this` or when using a multiline code block without language, like ``` this ``` which is the same as using `plain`, `text`, and probably others that apply the classes `cm-monospace cm-monospace` to the code lines. ### Reproducing On any document, on dark or light modes, create an inline word or sentence using the backtick character like 
`this`
or with triple backtick without language or using `plain` or `text` for a multiline code block, like 
```plain
this plain block
```
or 
```text
this text block
```
or 
```
this non-language code block
```
and after creating any of these elements, select characters or words, and they won't be highlighted. ### Zettlr Version Right off the develop branch ### Specify version _No response_ ### Installation Method From the Website or GitHub ### Your Platform - [ ] Windows - [X] macOS - [X] Linux ### Architecture - [X] Intel 64bit - [X] ARM 64bit ### Operating System Version Linux (Ubuntu 23.04) and macOS (Ventura 13) ### Additional Information Screenshots: not highlighting: ![not highlighting](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/assets/7761960/7c52ce62-489f-48f0-a18d-6ed696dc2b55) classes applying ![claases applying](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/assets/7761960/36e5415c-12b2-489b-a0bc-a0a8d8e73267) I will post a PR with CSS changes to the theme `berlin`. I understand I can apply my own CSS classes to Zettlr. However, the fix I was able to create won't work ok both dark and light themes simultaneously. This is my snippet for the light theme (which doesn't work on the dark theme) ```css :root { --green-selection: rgba(180, 240, 170, 0.6); --green-selection-dark: rgba( 90, 170, 80, 0.6); --blue-selection: rgba(200, 220, 240, 0.6); --blue-selection-dark: rgba( 29, 55, 134, 0.6); } body .main-editor-wrapper .cm-editor .code-block-line-background, body .main-editor-wrapper .cm-editor .cm-monospace { background-color: rgba(238, 238, 238, 0.5); } ``` Thank you.

## Description

Okay, I think it's time to make this PR a tad more visible. **~~This PR is not yet ready for review!~~ It's ready now!** You may, however, if you're curious, build it and run it in test mode (`yarn test-gui --clean`) to have a first look.

I must admit, I got really pissed with tons of pieces of the codebase and decided to clean up even completely unrelated parts (such as the FSAL). That explains the hefty amount of changed files (188 at the time of writing).

Was this a clever decision?

No. Not at all.

Was it a satisfying action?

You bet!

## Changes

The following ist is guaranteed to be incomplete, but I'll give it a shot:

1. Exchanged Codemirror 5 with Codemirror 6 (currently: Only the main editor, still todo: the smaller code editors)
2. While migrating the plugins from 5->6 gave most of them a facelift, thanks to, first, a MUCH better API, and second, a godsent AST
3. **Documents are now managed CENTRALLY in the DocumentsProvider**. Why does that matter? Because now there is **one** source of truth for all documents. This makes the shutdown logic much simpler, keeps every editor leaf in sync, and a ton of other benefits. Plus, it's fancy!
4. I finally decided to merge the Meta-descriptors in the FSAL with the "regular" ones. The distinction into meta and non-meta descriptors didn't make sense and is 100% on me. Now it's much better.
5. Disentangled the FSAL, Documents, and Target providers which previously were much tighter entangled. This had the problem of a thousand and one hard-to-track side-effects between the providers.
6. The FSAL now specifically has two utility functions that will return a descriptor for a random path on disk (as long as it's supported) and one that will return a (supported) file's contents. No more poking around in the guts of a provider.

## Additional information

Still TODO:

- [x] Move the CodeEditor to Codemirror 6
- [x] Re-implement the TableEditor
- [x] Allow for granular control of the renderers (currently hardcoded due to limitations in my imagination regarding the new config system)
- [x] Put the extensions into compartments for easy-ish reconfiguration of existing documents
- [x] Re-enable spell-checking
- [x] Implement a Markdown linter since that re-uses 99% of the code required for the new spell checker and because I can.
- [x] Write a few code-fold extensions to allow for better code folding, even where we're limited to the older `StreamParser`s
- [x] Better styling for the tooltips (which are now no longer sponsored by tippy but by the library itself)
- [x] Better styling for the autocompletes (strictly speaking also "only" tooltips)
- [x] Re-implement the emphasis-renderer because now we can do that properly (thanks to the AST)
- [x] More careful (secure) inline-HTML rendering (since we can do that now, thanks to the AST)
- [x] Re-implement iFrame rendering
- [x] When the user closes the last tab, empty out the editor
- [x] Add an exemption for the autocorrect to not replace --- with an em dash when it's the first line of the file (collides with YAML frontmatter)
- [x] Spellchecker currently takes sometimes more than a minute to check a word (due to the overhead of approx. 50-70ms per IPC call), I need to bundle multiple words together (basically: string array in, array of booleans out)
- [x] Implement `==.+==`-highlighting
- [x] Fix styles of emphasis/strong emphasis in and outside of lists

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: macOS 12.6 (Silicon/ARM)
